question: Did the applicant include a Project Narrative?
complianceGuidance: The Project Narrative must be attached in the Project Narrative form by clicking the Attach File button. The User Guide requires the Project Narrative to be present as an uploaded document in the application. The reviewer should confirm the presence of the Project Narrative attachment in the application table of contents. The absence of this document results in non-compliance.
description: Checks for inclusion of the Project Narrative attachment.

question: Public Agencies: Does the application include Attachment 6: Co-Applicant Agreement?
complianceGuidance: Attachment 6: Co-Applicant Agreement is required for public agency applicants as indicated on Form 1A. The reviewer must confirm that this attachment is present if the applicant is a public agency. If the applicant is not a public agency, this requirement is not applicable.
description: Checks for inclusion of Co-Applicant Agreement for public agencies.

question: New applicant: Does the application include Attachment 11: Evidence of Nonprofit or Public Agency Status?
lookFor: Attachment 11: Evidence of Nonprofit, Public Agency, or Tribal or Urban Indian Organization Status
complianceGuidance: Attachment 11 is required for new applicants (Type 1) to demonstrate nonprofit, public agency, or Tribal/Urban Indian organization status. The reviewer must confirm the presence of this attachment for new applicants. If the applicant is not new, this requirement is not applicable.
description: Checks for inclusion of evidence of nonprofit/public agency status for new applicants.

question: New and Competing Supplement applicants: Does the application include Attachment 12: Operational Plan?
complianceGuidance: Attachment 12: Operational Plan is required for new (Type 1) and competing supplement (Type 3) applicants. The reviewer must confirm the presence of this attachment for these applicant types. If the applicant is not new or competing supplement, this requirement is not applicable.
description: Checks for inclusion of Operational Plan for new and competing supplement applicants.

question: New applicant: Is the applicant a private, nonprofit entity or a public agency (including Tribal, and urban Indian organizations) in the United States?
suggestedResources: Attachment 11: Evidence of Nonprofit or Public Agency Status
lookFor: Attachment 11: Evidence of Nonprofit, Public Agency, or Tribal or Urban Indian Organization Status
complianceGuidance: New applicants must be either a private, nonprofit entity or a public agency (including Tribal and urban Indian organizations) in the United States. The reviewer should verify that Attachment 11 provides evidence of the applicant's eligible entity type. If the applicant is not new, this requirement is not applicable.
description: Checks entity eligibility for new applicants.

question: Does the applicant propose to perform a substantive role in the project (i.e., does not apply on behalf of another organization)?
suggestedResources: Budget Narrative | Attachment 2: Bylaws | Form 5A: Services Provided | Form 8: Health Center Agreements
complianceGuidance: The applicant must demonstrate a substantive role in the project and not apply on behalf of another organization. The reviewer should confirm that the applicant is listed as the primary entity in the Budget Narrative, Bylaws, and Form 5A, and that Form 8 does not indicate that another entity is carrying out the majority of the scope. If the applicant is only serving as a pass-through or fiscal agent, this is non-compliant.
description: Checks that applicant is not applying on behalf of another organization.

question: Does the applicant propose to operate a health center that makes all required primary health care services available and accessible in the service area, either directly or through established arrangements, without regard for ability to pay? (Note that applicants may not propose to provide ONLY a single service or any subset of the required primary health care services.)
complianceGuidance: The applicant must propose to make all required primary health care services available and accessible in the service area, either directly or through formal written arrangements, and must not limit services to only a single service or subset. Form 5A must show all required services provided via Column I, II, or III, and the Project Narrative must describe accessibility regardless of ability to pay.
description: Checks for provision of all required primary health care services.

question: Does the applicant propose to make General Primary Medical Care available directly (Column I) and/or through formal written contractual agreements in which the health center pays for the service (Column II)?
complianceGuidance: Form 5A must indicate that General Primary Medical Care is available either directly (Column I) or through formal written contractual agreements (Column II). The reviewer should confirm that at least one of these columns is checked for General Primary Medical Care. Referral arrangements (Column III) alone are not sufficient.
description: Checks that General Primary Medical Care is available via direct provision or contract.

question: Does the applicant propose to make services accessible to all? If a sub-population is targeted (e.g., a school-based site that targets school-aged children), will health center services be available and accessible to others seeking services at the proposed site(s)?
suggestedResources: Project Narrative | Form 5A: Services Provided | Project Abstract
complianceGuidance: Evaluate whether the applicant's service model demonstrates accessibility to all individuals, not just a targeted sub-population. Consider the TOTALITY of evidence across multiple sources: (1) Project Narrative describing populations served and service delivery model (e.g., fixed sites, mobile units, outreach), (2) Form 5A showing full-scope required services provided, (3) Project Abstract describing target populations served. If a sub-population is targeted (e.g., school-based sites for school-aged children), answer 'Yes' if the evidence shows services extend beyond that sub-population — such as serving families, homeless individuals, public housing residents, community members, or providing mobile/outreach services to the broader community. Answer 'No' ONLY if the application restricts services exclusively to a single sub-population with no indication of broader access to others seeking care at the proposed site(s).
description: Checks for accessibility of services to all, not just sub-populations.

question: Does the applicant propose a service area announced under this NOFO number? If the answer to Question 10 is "No", select "N/A" for Questions 11 through 15.
suggestedResources: Summary Page | SAAT | Project Abstract | Project Narrative | Form 5B: Service Sites
complianceGuidance: The applicant must propose to serve a service area listed in the Service Area Announcement Table (SAAT) for the current NOFO. The reviewer should verify that the Service Area ID, city, and state on the Summary Page and Form 5B match an entry in the SAAT. If not, mark Questions 11-15 as N/A.
description: Checks that the proposed service area matches the NOFO SAAT.

question: Is the total number of unduplicated patients projected to be served in the assessment period on Form 1A at least 75 percent of the SAAT Patient Target?
complianceGuidance: The total unduplicated patient projection for the assessment period (CY 2028) on Form 1A must be at least 75 percent of the Patient Target listed in the SAAT for the proposed service area. The reviewer should compare the Form 1A projection to the SAAT Patient Target and confirm the 75% threshold is met.
description: Checks that patient projection meets 75% of SAAT Patient Target.

question: Does the applicant propose to serve patients for each Service Type listed in the SAAT?
complianceGuidance: For each Service Type listed in the SAAT for the service area, Form 1A must include patient projections for the assessment period. The reviewer should confirm that Form 1A includes projections for all required Service Types from the SAAT; zeros are acceptable for service types not listed in the SAAT.
description: Checks that patient projections are provided for all SAAT Service Types.

question: Does the applicant request annual SAC funding that does NOT exceed the SAAT Total Funding for the service area?
complianceGuidance: The total federal funding requested in SF-424A Section A-C must not exceed the Total Funding available for the service area as listed in the SAAT. The reviewer should compare the requested amount to the SAAT and confirm it does not exceed the maximum.
description: Checks that funding request does not exceed SAAT Total Funding.

question: Does the applicant maintain the current funding distribution (i.e., CHC, MSAW, HP, RPH) from the SAAT?
complianceGuidance: Funding must be requested for each population type (CHC, MSAW, HP, RPH) that has an amount greater than $0 in the SAAT, and the funding distribution across population types must be maintained. The reviewer should confirm that the applicant's requested funding per population type matches the SAAT distribution.
description: Checks that funding distribution matches SAAT population types.

question: Does the applicant propose to serve patients for each population type listed in the SAAT?
suggestedResources: SF-424A | SAAT | Form 1A: General Information Worksheet
complianceGuidance: For each population type listed in the SAAT with funding greater than $0, Form 1A must include patient projections for the assessment period. The reviewer should confirm that Form 1A includes projections for all population types corresponding to the funding requested in SF-424A.
description: Checks that patient projections are provided for all SAAT population types.

question: New or competing supplement applicant: For Form 5B: Service Sites, does the applicant include: · Service area zip codes where the zip code patient percentages on the SAAT total at least 75 percent; or . All service area zip codes if the sum of all zip code patient percentages on the SAAT is less than 75 percent?
complianceGuidance: Form 5B must include zip codes for at least 75 percent of current patients served, based on the SAAT zip code patient percentages. If the sum of all zip code patient percentages in the SAAT is less than 75 percent, all zip codes from the SAAT must be entered. The reviewer should confirm compliance by comparing Form 5B zip codes to SAAT data.
description: Checks that zip codes in Form 5B meet SAAT 75% threshold or include all SAAT zip codes.

question: New or competing supplement applicant: Is at least one new full-time (operational 40 hours or more per week) permanent, fixed building site with a verifiable street address proposed on Form 5B? * If funding is only requested for MSAW, the applicant may propose a new full-time seasonal (rather than permanent) service delivery site.
complianceGuidance: New and competing supplement applicants must propose at least one new Service Delivery or Administrative/Service Delivery site with Location Type 'Permanent' and operating at least 40 hours per week with a verifiable street address. If only MSAW funding is requested, a new full-time seasonal site is acceptable. The reviewer should confirm site type, hours, and address in Form 5B.
description: Checks for at least one new full-time permanent site (or seasonal for MSAW) in Form 5B.

question: New or competing supplement applicant requesting RPH funding: Does the applicant demonstrate consultation with residents of public housing in the preparation of the SAC application and ensure ongoing consultation with the residents regarding the planning and administration of the health center?
complianceGuidance: Applicants requesting RPH funding must demonstrate in the Project Narrative that consultation with residents of public housing occurred during preparation of the application and that ongoing consultation will be ensured for planning and administration. The reviewer should look for explicit statements and examples of consultation.
description: Checks for evidence of consultation with public housing residents for RPH funding.

question: New or competing supplement applicant requesting HP and/or RPH funding: Does the applicant attest on the Summary Page that funding will be utilized to supplement, and not supplant, the expenditures of the health center and the value of in-kind contributions for the delivery of services to these populations?
complianceGuidance: Applicants requesting HP and/or RPH funding must attest on the Summary Page that grant funds will supplement, not supplant, health center expenditures and in-kind contributions for services to these populations. The reviewer should confirm the attestation is checked and a description is provided. If the Summary Page 'Not Applicable' checkbox is checked (indicating the organization is submitting a competing continuation application, or is a new/competing supplement applicant NOT requesting HP and/or RPH funding), this question is N/A.
description: Checks for attestation of supplement/not supplant for HP/RPH funding.

question: If the Form 1A patient projection is less than 95% of the SAAT Patient Target, is the annual SAC funding request reduced accordingly in the SF-424A and Budget Narrative? * This question does not impact application completeness or eligibility.
suggestedResources: SF-424A | Budget Narrative | SAAT | Patient and Budget Calculator
complianceGuidance: If the Form 1A patient projection is between 75% and 94.9% of the SAAT Patient Target, the annual SAC funding request in SF-424A and Budget Narrative must be reduced proportionally, as described in the NOFO and User Guide. The reviewer should confirm the requested funding is less than the SAAT amount and matches the reduction percentage.
description: Checks for proportional funding reduction if patient projection is less than 95% of SAAT target.

question: Is the application complete based on the required documents mentioned in the Completeness and Eligibility Checklist?
complianceGuidance: The application is considered complete if all required documents and forms listed in the Completeness and Eligibility Checklist are present and meet the criteria described in the User Guide. The reviewer should summarize prior answers to confirm completeness.
description: Checks overall completeness based on all prior checklist items.

question: Based on BPHC OPPD review, is the applicant eligible?
complianceGuidance: Applicant eligibility is determined based on compliance with all requirements in the Completeness and Eligibility Checklist, including entity type, substantive role, service area, patient projections, funding, and all other criteria described in the User Guide. The reviewer should summarize prior answers to determine eligibility.
description: Checks overall eligibility based on all prior compliance answers.
